lines: Türkiye somehow contrived to lose to Paraguay, leaving The Foreman with zero points from his Turkish adventure — and today Ecuador await his beloved Curaçao, a team with roughly the same World Cup pedigree as a pub side from Wolverhampton.

Al watched the Netherlands batter Sweden 5-1 yesterday, which was lovely for him and absolutely devastating for Jim, who now has Sweden limping on 3 points while his Qatar side are barely breathing in Group B — Jim is essentially running a retirement home for footballing nations.

The Morocco train keeps rolling — Butters, Callum, Jim and Wilmot all pocketed points from the Scotland win, but with four of them sharing the same golden ticket, it's less a competitive advantage and more a timeshare in Marrakech.

Today's match to watch: Tunisia vs Japan in Group F — Callum and Dene desperately need a win to get off the mark, and if Tunisia bottle it, Dene will be propping up the entire sweepstake on zero points with Bosnia also going nowhere fast.
